data.content_ref: inline:{"claim": "Harness strictly beats self-consistency only in the mid-zone error regime where the majority is fragile: base candidate error e must satisfy 1/(k+1) < e < k/(k+1) so that some correct majorities are breakable, AND critic recall on wrong candidates must exceed the break-rate of correct majorities (recall_wrong > falseflag_rate_correct), with critic precision on wrong candidates > 0.5. Outside this zone harness ties or loses.", "mechanism": "Self-consistency relies on majority frequency. When e is very low (as in observed data), correct majorities are super-stable (4/5 or 5/5 right) and filtering can only break them via false flags → net harm or zero. When e is very high, majorities are already wrong and filtering cannot recover without a near-perfect critic. The strict-benefit zone is where majorities are 3/5 or 3/7 — correct but fragile — so a critic with recall_wrong > falseflag_rate_correct removes enough wrong answers to flip 3/5→2/3 (benefit) while false-flaging few enough correct answers to avoid 3/5→2/3 (harm). The net benefit is recall_wrong × P(fragile-correct) − falseflag_rate_correct × P(fragile-correct), which is positive only in the mid-zone.", "scope": {"covers": ["odd k ≥ 5", "candidate error e in (1/(k+1), k/(k+1))", "critic with recall_wrong > falseflag_rate_correct and precision_wrong > 0.5"], "excludes": ["e ≤ 1/(k+1) (stable correct majorities, observed easy-set regime)", "e ≥ k/(k+1) (stable wrong majorities)", "even k (tie-breaking complications)"]}, "forbidden": [{"case": "At k=7, e≈0.33 (mid-zone), critic recall_wrong=0.6 and falseflag_rate_correct=0.1: harness accuracy is LOWER than or EQUAL to self-consistency despite recall > falseflag rate", "eval": "rubric:std-crit-design"}, {"case": "At k=5, e≤0.15 (low-zone, observed): harness accuracy strictly exceeds self-consistency", "eval": "rubric:std-crit-design"}, {"case": "At k=7, e≈0.33, a question-adaptive critic with identical recall_wrong and falseflag_rate_correct as a fixed critic yields different harness accuracy, contradicting the mechanism that only aggregate rates matter", "eval": "rubric:std-crit-design"}], "prose_notes": "This is the mechanistic base-case: a single fixed-precision critic operating in the mid-zone. It refutes the observed-data extrapolation (which lives in the low-error tail) by showing the strict-benefit zone is structurally inaccessible at e≤0.05. The decisive experiment sweeps k∈{5,7,9} across e∈{0.15,0.33,0.55} with a controlled critic (recall 0.6, falseflag 0.1), using 120 items × 5 seeds = 600 samples per cell, ~270k tokens total — fitting the 300k budget."}
